mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust purchasing power without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when RSAFS increases and CPIAUCSL remains flat, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product confirm sustained economic growth and corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day equity returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor market strength and residential investment demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 20-day forward returns over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low federal funds rates signals accommodative monetary policy amplifying supply-side growth, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, with INDPRO growth showing no statistically significant relationship to 60-day equity returns.
